publishedAbstract: In some instances, it may be necessary to migrate to different Ceph Object Storage Daemons (OSDs). OSDs are the backing devices (disks) Ceph pools/storageclasses use to store data. Common reasons for this operation are as follows: 1. Datastore is getting full/moving to a new datastore. If this scenario aligns with the planned migration of your environment, this is the correct solution. 2. Migrating to a differently sized/type of OSD, or simply executing an expedited redeployment. If this scenario aligns with the intended migration plan, please use the [Migrating Ceph Object Storage Daemons (OSDs) / Disks in OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF)- Using the Same Storageclass (Migrating Device Size/Type)](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7134635) solution. 3. Expanding on [2], each OSD, regardless of size, uses 2 CPU and 5GiB of memory in most deployments. Reference [7.3. Resource requirements](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_openshift_data_foundation/4.20/html-single/planning_your_deployment/index#resource-requirements_rhodf) of the Product Documentation for more information. Many small devices can become costly/inefficient when disks can be consolidated to a bigger device, eliminating the need to consume unnecessary resources for the same amount of storage space. **WARNING: This process, if followed correctly, is fairly straightforward and safe. However, take extra precautions during the removal of the older OSDs. OSDs should ONLY be removed one at a time, allowing for rebalancing between removals. Ceph should be allowed to progress to a state of HEALTH_OK, all PGs active+clean (scrubbing is OK) before removing the next OSD, and so on. Failure to do so and executing the rapid removal of OSDs before rebalancing is allowed to finish can result in complete data loss!!!!**
publishedTitle: Migrating Ceph Object Storage Daemons (OSDs) / Disks in OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF) to a New Storageclass (Migrating Datastore/Local Storage Backing Devices)
